title: Ultimate Fatty Tender Greek Lamb Tagine
description: A show-off Greek dish featuring shio-koji, deglaze, and a tender, fatty lamb, served in a traditional cataplana.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a cataplana over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the shio-koji, oregano, thyme, red wine, chicken broth, tomato paste, honey, salt, and pepper.
Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the cataplana from the heat and let it sit, covered, for 10 minutes.
Serve the lamb hot, garnished with fresh herbs and crusty bread on the side.
Deglaze the cataplana with a little water and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Strain the sauce before serving to remove any solids.
